response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo's career is overwhelmingly TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang development with web, mobile, and embedded systems focus. The role demands 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows, strong SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, and capital markets or fixed-income domain knowledge. C++ appears in only a six-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — totaling well under a year. The one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) involved Node.js and TypeScript in a German retail investment fintech context, not capital markets or fixed income. There is no evidence of Windows-platform development, SQL Server or Oracle use, Summit platform experience, or any fixed-income domain exposure.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience handling millions of requests per second using async I/O, caching, and low-latency design at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Broad CI/CD and DevOps depth including GitLab, Docker, Artifactory, trunk-based development, and deploy automation across multiple roles
Demonstrated polyglot adaptability spanning DLang, TypeScript, Java, C#, Swift, and Node.js across a wide range of domains
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project; the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ is entirely absent
No SQL Server or Oracle experience documented; SQL appears only as a generic skill listing with no substantive database-heavy work described

No capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain knowledge — the RIDE Capital role was retail fintech built on Node.js/TypeScript, not trading or fixed-income infrastructure
